message:
fix(abi): add validity-bit guards to rwlockattr and barrierattr, reject use-after-destroy

rwlockattr changes:
- Introduce RWLOCKATTR_VALID_BIT, encode_rwlockattr(), decode_rwlockattr_pshared()
  helpers to pack/unpack pshared alongside a validity sentinel.
- pthread_rwlockattr_init now stores the encoded word (valid + PROCESS_PRIVATE).
- pthread_rwlockattr_getpshared and _setpshared check the valid bit and
  return EINVAL on a destroyed attr (zeroed memory).

barrierattr changes:
- Introduce BARRIERATTR_VALID_BIT constant.
- pthread_barrierattr_init stores VALID_BIT | PROCESS_PRIVATE.
- pthread_barrierattr_getpshared validates the bit before reading; masks it
  before returning the pshared value.
- pthread_barrierattr_setpshared validates the bit before writing.

Tests:
- rwlockattr_getpshared_after_destroy_is_rejected: init → destroy → get → EINVAL.
- barrierattr_getpshared_after_destroy_is_rejected: init → destroy → get → EINVAL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
